title: Golden Crisp Middle Eastern Rice Pilaf
description: A sweet and tangy rice pilaf dish with a crunchy topping, perfect for a romantic dinner.
instructions: Heat butter in a large saucepan over medium heat. Add the ground cinnamon and ginger and sauté for 1 minute.
Add the fried-grind-rice-khao-khua and stir to coat with the butter mixture.
Add the lemon juice and stir well to combine.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and cover.
Simmer for 15 minutes, or until the rice is cooked and the liquid has been absorbed.
In a small pan, melt the golden caster sugar over medium heat, stirring constantly.
Once the sugar is golden brown, remove from heat and stir in the raisins and dried apricots (if using).
Top the cooked rice pilaf with the crispy sugar mixture.
Serve warm, garnished with additional raisins and dried apricots if desired.
